2001-05-01.title: Bush Commits U.S. to a Missile Defense System
2001-05-01.abstract: WASHINGTON, May 1 — President Bush committed the United States today to building a missile defense system for a world in which cold-war thinking is outmoded and the Soviet Union is fading into memory.

2001-05-01.title: 8 Palestinians and 1 Israeli Die in Surge Of Violence in West Bank and Gaza
2001-05-01.abstract: Seven Palestinians are killed in West Bank and Gaza, some from bombs that may have been intended for Israelis, and eighth is shot and killed overnight by Israeli troops near Qalqilya; Israeli motorist is killed in shooting attack near settlement in West Bank (S)

2001-05-01.title: World Briefing/AFRICA: ZIMBABWE: TRIAL SET FOR OPPOSITION LEADER
2001-05-01.abstract: The leader of the opposition Movement for Democratic Change, Morgan Tsvangirai, will stand trial next week for a statement that government officials say could have led to the overthrow of the government. The charges stem from a speech last year in which Mr. Tsvangirai warned President Robert Mugabe that if he did not leave power peacefully he would be removed violently. If convicted, Mr. Tsvangirai could be sentenced to life in prison.     Rachel L. Swarns (NYT)

2001-05-01.title: Parliament In Indonesia Votes Censure Of President
2001-05-01.abstract: Angry Indonesian Parliament votes overwhelmingly to censure Pres Abdurrahman Wahid, moving big step closer to impeachment proceedings and heightening calls for his resignation; raucous and hostile body that he once dismissed as kindergarten voted, 353 to 52 to censure him for corruption and incompetence; photo (M)

2001-05-02.title: Death and Daily Life Link Arab and Israeli
2001-05-02.abstract: Assaf Hershkovitz, 31-year-old Jewish settler, is shot to death by Palestinian gunmen while driving to work; is killed despite bullet-proof vest he began wearing after his father was murdered on same West Bank road in January; photo of funeral; Palestinians mourn two children and 24-year-old Hassan Qadi, who died when explosion leveled Ramallah home; Qadi, who belonged to Yasir Arafat's Fatah organization, was suspected of killing Israeli lured to death through Internet relationship with Palestinian woman (M)

2001-05-02.title: In Twist, Rebels Help Drug Effort In Colombia
2001-05-02.abstract: FARC rebels in Colombia's mountains are apparently deeply involved in talks between government and farmers who have agreed in principle to replace poppy fields with legal crops, in exchange for subsidies and technical help; map; rebels, better known for collecting taxes on illegal crops, are said to be helping to shape programs to help farmers (M)

2001-05-02.title: In Bush's Words: 'Substantial Advantages of Intercepting Missiles Early'
2001-05-02.abstract: Excerpt from Pres Bush's speech on international military policy, including proposed missile defense system (M)

2001-05-02.title: Victims Taken From Ship Confirm Their Status : 'Slave' Children's Plight
2001-05-02.abstract: GENEVA—  Children found on a ship that drew the world's attention to the modern-day African child slave trade have made statements indicating they were victims of illegal trafficking, according to the Swiss-based aid group caring for them.

2001-05-02.title: World Briefing  Middle East: Israel: Police Question Barak
2001-05-02.abstract: Israreli police question former Prime Min Ehud Barak regarding possibility of illegal campaign financing in 1999 election (S)

2001-05-03.title: First Pigeons, Now Cars. Assisi Is Up in Arms Again.
2001-05-03.abstract: Assisi, medieval hill town that was badly damaged by 1997 earthquake, is experiencing new problems over city ordinance to allow some automobile traffic in front of Basilica of St Francis of Assisi; under aegis of Unesco, square was repaved with stones donated from five continents; opponents of decision say cars would spoil beauty of place (M)

2001-05-03.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Iraq: No Cooperation On Missing People
2001-05-03.abstract: Iraq reiterates refusal to cooperate with UN in determining fate of about 600 Kuwaitis and citizens of other countries reported missing since invasion of Kuwait in 1990 (S)

2001-05-03.title: Central Asia Braces to Fight Islamic Rebels
2001-05-03.abstract: Former Soviet republics try to strengthen their armies to thwart annual attacks by radical Islamic insurgents financed from Afghanistan and operating with ever greater precision and sophistication from remote mountain bases; shadowy rebels are known as Islamic Movement of Uzbekistan; they have mounted deadly raids into Kyrgyzstan and Uzbekistan, aiming to put shaky states on defensive and to carry their cause gradually eastward, toward repressed Muslim minorities of western China; these guerrillas are latest armed band to emerge from ruins of conflict between American-backed rebels and Russians in Afghanistan; rebels' leader is Juma Namangani, who became radicalized during service as Soviet paratrooper in Afghanistan and trained opposition soldiers in civil war in Tajikistan; rebels' goal is to carve Islamic state from territory of some Central Asian countries; estimates of guerrillas' strength range from few hundred to several thousand; for now, biggest fear is expanded fighting will create thousands of refugees, increasing ethnic tensions among Kyrgyz, Tajiks and Uzbeks who live side by side; photos; map (L)

2001-05-03.title: Manila Moves Incite Talk of 'Dark Side of People Power'
2001-05-03.abstract: Pres Gloria Macapagal Arroyo of Philippines uses 'state of rebellion' as reason to widen crackdown on opposition leaders, whom she acuses of inciting violence; this leaves even her own supporters fearing that abrupt ouster of her predecessor, Joseph Estrada, was dangerous precedent; ouster occurred before Estrada's impeachment trial on corruption charges had reached verdict; Aquilino Pimentel, president of Senate, says fear now is that massing million people on streets can topple any government; says country cannot afford to have such 'people power'; photo (M)

2001-05-03.title: Rumsfeld's Office Reverses China Ban
2001-05-03.abstract: Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld's office orders suspension of military exchanges and contacts with Chinese armed forces and then abruptly reverses order after White House objects; unusual Pentagon retraction reflects degree of confusion in admininstration that tries to project disciplined management style; it underscores divisions among Pres Bush's advisers over how tough to be with China after confrontation over American surveillance plane that remains at Chinese military base on Hainan Island (M)

2001-05-04.title: In Moving to Shun China, Bush Team Trips Over Itself
2001-05-04.abstract: For the second time in eight days the White House tried today to explain miscues in its China policy, insisting that President Bush and his national security team had decided weeks ago to evaluate all government contacts with China case by case, despite a memorandum to the contrary written by a top assistant to Defense Secretary Donald H. Rumsfeld.    The memorandum, issued and then retracted within hours by the Pentagon on Wednesday, had ordered the suspension of military exchanges and contacts with the Chinese armed forces. Today Mr. Bush himself tried to clarify the policy, while spokesmen at the White House and the Pentagon tried to explain how a seemingly clear directive from the President was turned on its head inside Mr. Rumsfeld's office.

2001-05-04.title: Blair Says Cattle Disease Is Near End; Elections Due
2001-05-04.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair says that Britain is in home stretch of its race to contain ruinous epidemic of foot-and-mouth disease, prompting widepread forecasts that he is on verge of calling general election in June (M)

2001-05-04.title: Elite French College Tackles Affirmative Action
2001-05-04.abstract: Institut d'Etudes Politiques, long associated with France's ruling elite, announces affirmative action program, first such effort by any French college; decision to create just 20 slots for students from disadvantaged areas, where many residents are immigrants, causes uproar in press; France has long embraced concept that admission strictly based on test results is best way to level playing field between rich and poor; photo (M)

2001-05-04.title: Bush Condemns Suppression Of Religious Freedom in Sudan
2001-05-04.abstract: President Bush tonight delivered his most strongly worded condemnation of countries that seek to suppress religious freedom, particularly singling out Sudan and declaring that ''my administration will continue to speak and act for as long as persecution and atrocities in Sudan last.''    In a speech to the American Jewish Committee, Mr. Bush said that ''at the first meeting of my National Security Council, I told them a top foreign policy priority is the safety and security of Israel.''

2001-05-05.title: Iran's President Seeks New Term, Vowing More Political Change
2001-05-05.abstract: Iran Pres Mohammad Khatami registers as candidate for re-election, ending months of speculation; gives emotional speech vowing to continue efforts to carry out political and social changes despite fierce conservative opposition (S)

2001-05-05.title: Tokyo Journal; Ryotei: Vanishing Havens of the High and Mighty
2001-05-05.abstract: Tokyo Journal on dwingling number of ryotei, exclusive restaurants and gathering places where high and mighty Japanese men have traditionally gathered to conclude secret deals and relax in company of geisha; Noriko Suzuki offers rare glimpse of Kacho ryotei she runs; photo (M)

2001-05-05.title: North Korea Refuses to Stop Arms Exports, Delegation Says
2001-05-05.abstract: Javier Solana, European Union's top security official, holds North Korea's leader, Kim Jong Il, refuses to renounce exports of missiles and missile technology; notes that European Union delegation meeting with Kim had been equally firm in emphasizing EU's opposition to such exports (M)

2001-05-05.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: No Foot-and-Mouth In Humans
2001-05-05.abstract: British health authorities say tests on 21 people suspected of having foot-and-mouth disease are negative (S)8

2001-05-05.title: U.S. Student Says Russia Tried to Recruit Him

2001-05-06.title: To Weather Recession, Argentines Revert to Barter
2001-05-06.abstract: By the standards of most Latin American countries, Pedro Pérez hardly looks like a charity case. He wears a handsome sports watch and a thick gold wedding ring. His hair is neatly parted, he has all his teeth and his meticulous handwriting is the product of a decent public school education.     But Mr. Pérez is just scraping by, struggling like many other Argentines to hold on to a middle-class life three years into a deep recession. At 43, he cannot count on a regular salary from his sales job at a shoe factory anymore, so he has been forced to sell his town house and Ford sedan, and his wife has gone back to work.

2001-05-06.title: China's Ban Of Magazine Clouds Forum In Hong Kong
2001-05-06.abstract: Fortune magazine is sponsoring Fortune Global Forum in Hong Kong, bringing together corporate titans and former Pres Bill Clinton, but conference is marred for Fortune's parent company, AOL Time Warner, because newsstand sales of Time magazine, another unit, have been banned in China since early March, shortly after magazine published article on Falun Gong spiritual movement in Hong Kong; Falun Gong, which is banned in China but not in Hong Kong, will hold demonstration three blocks from conference to protest speech by Pres Jiang Zemin (M)

2001-05-06.title: At Home, Mexico Mistreats Its Migrant Farmhands
2001-05-06.abstract: Article in series examining third-world production for first-world markets focuses on plight of Mexico's migrant farmhands, who abandon homes for part of year to harvest crops, exported mostly to US, and endure dangerous and unsanitary conditions; about 200,000 who settle in nearly 150 work camps across Sinaloa state are among poorest of Mexico's 40 million poor; half are women and girls, and at least 30 percent are children under 15; they travel mostly as families or entire communities, and almost 40 percent are Indians who do not speak Spanish well; even though Pres Vicente Fox has been outspoken advocate of Mexican laborers in US, pressing Washington to improve their working conditions, he has not devoted attention in five months in office to cares of migrant workers at home, even following recent discovery that young children work at his family's plants packing vegetables; photos; map (M)

2001-05-06.title: Water in Tap Beats Bottled, Group Says
2001-05-06.abstract: World Wide Fund for Nature says that bottled water is no purer or safer than tap water and demands annual production of 1.5 million tons of plastic bottles, whose manufacture and disposal can release gases that contribute to climate change; says increasing popularity of bottled water also threatens to erode regulatory standards for tap water (M)

2001-05-06.title: U.S. Accused Of Diluting a Global Pact to Limit Use of Tobacco
2001-05-06.abstract: No progress is reported at meeting in Geneva where governments from more than 150 countries are trying to work out provisions of treaty that World Health Organization is pushing to stem rising death toll around the world from tobacco-related diseases; US and other major countries are now backpedaling on proposed complete ban on cigarette advertising and promotion; antismoking activists accuse US and Japan of siding with tobacco multinationals seeking to dilute treaty language (M)

2001-05-07.title: To European Eyes, It's America the Ugly
2001-05-07.abstract: A torrent of hostile articles in Europe's press have focused on American foreign policy stances in President Bush's first 100 days, but any missteps by the Bush administration provided ammunition for a gun that was already partly loaded.    News analysis, Page A10.

2001-05-07.title: Billion Here, Billion There: One Italian's Big Problem
2001-05-07.abstract: Silvio Berlusconi, media tycoon running for prime minister of Italy, has dinner with fellow media baron, Rupert Murdoch, fueling speculation that Berlusconi might be trying to sell his controlling interest in all three of Italy's private television networks; Berlusconi later announces that he will appoint panel of international experts to propose solution to conflict of interest presented by his financial empire; it is not clear whether Murdoch would be one of experts (M)

2001-05-07.title: U.S. Resumes Reconnaissance Flights Off China's Coast
2001-05-07.abstract: WASHINGTON, May 7 — The United States resumed reconnaissance flights off the coast of China today for the first time since an April 1 collision between a Navy intelligence-gathering plane and a Chinese fighter bruised relations between the two countries, defense and military officials said.

2001-05-07.title: POPE, IN DAMASCUS, GOES TO A MOSQUE IN MOVE FOR UNITY
2001-05-07.abstract: John Paul II becomes first pope ever to set foot in mosque, in visit to Umayyad Mosque in Damascus, Syria; pope is making bold bid to cut through centuries of divisions, but local leaders make even bolder efforts to press Roman Catholic Church to make common cause against Judaism; anti-Semitic remarks made to pope by Muhammad Ziyadah, Syria's minister of religious affairs, and earlier by Syria's president, Bashar al-Assad, infuriate officials in Israel and Jewish groups in United States; friction continues to punctuate pope's six-day pilgrimage to Greece, Syria and Malta to retrace steps of Apostle Paul; in Damascus, pope listens silently to speeches that mix hopeful calls of religious understanding with fierce oratory of war that rules Middle East; photo of pope with Sheik Ahmad Kuftaro, grand mufti of Syria (M)

2001-05-07.title: Pope Prays for Peace in Destroyed Golan Heights City
2001-05-07.abstract: QUNEITRA, Syria, May 7 -- Pouring water on a potted olive tree, Pope John Paul II prayed today for peace and forgiveness in the Middle East as he visited a shattered city on the Golan Heights that is mainly a symbol of irreconcilable conflict.

2001-05-08.title: World Briefing | Asia: China: Plea To Release Prisoners
2001-05-08.abstract: More than 100 academics based in Hong Kong sign petition calling on Pres Jiang Zemin of China, who is visiting Hong Kong, to release foreign scholars detained in China; one of scholars, Li Shaomin, is an American citizen based at City University of Hong Kong (S)

2001-05-08.title: Bump in Hanoi's Capitalist Road: Jockeying in U.S. Congress
2001-05-08.abstract: Wide-ranging trade pact between United States and Vietnam, signed last July after four years of hard lobbying and gentle persuasion, has now stalled in Washington, apparently victim of Vietnam's receding importance for United States; although agreement is still broadly supported in Congress, its passage is imperiled by plans to include it in omnibus trade package that could take months or years to approve; American officials who have make agreement centerpiece of their policy in Vietnam are mortified as Vietnamese ask vexing questions about reliability of Washington as partner and value of its promises; their concerns are shared by American business executives in Vietnam who have been waiting for gates to trade and investment to be opened fully; photo (M)

2001-05-08.title: World Briefing | Americas: Argentina: Unhappy with Castro
2001-05-08.abstract: Argentine government withdraws its ambassador to Cuba to protest comments by Pres Fidel Castro that characterized Pres Fernando de la Rua's government as 'bootlickers of the Yankees' (S)

2001-05-08.title: RUMSFELD SEEKING AN ARMS STRATEGY USING OUTER SPACE
2001-05-08.abstract: Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld will propose sweeping overhaul of Pentagon's space programs, sharply increasing importance of outer space in strategic planning; proposal will be first step toward making space focus of Pentagon spending, and is intended to emphasize importance Bush administration places on developing weapon systems for outer space; Rumsfeld wants to establish new Pentagon post for four-star Air Force general to serve as advocate for what could become new space force; Rumsfeld has expressed interest in developing costly and complicated space weapons systems, including lasers capable of shooting down ballistic missiles and satellites designed to attack other satellites; any efforts to build such weapons would be opposed by China, Russia and European nations, and by arms control advocates; space programs currently represent only $8 billion of Pentagon's $310 billion annual budget (M)

2001-05-08.title: John Paul Prays for Peace In Former War Zone in Syria
2001-05-08.abstract: Pope John Paul II prays for peace and forgiveness in Middle East in visit to Quneitra, shattered city on Golan Heights seen as symbol of irreconcilable conflict; pope is greeted by thousands of Syrians, many of them former residents, who stand silently in front of their flattened houses; they are brought by bus to greet him; Golan Heights were captured by Israel in 1967 Arab-Israeli war; Quneitra is portion returned to Syria in 1974 agreement brokered by United States; Syria has kept city uninhabited and in ruins as museum; pope's very presence in Quneitra provides showcase for Syrian government's efforts to portray him as their ally against Israel; map (M)

2001-05-09.title: Philippine Eruption Snuffed Out Tribe's Way of Life : Pinatubo:10 Years Later
2001-05-09.abstract: PATALBATO, Philippines—  The sky darkened and the ground shook. It sounded like a "stampede of 100,000 animals," said a Filipino who witnessed it.

2001-05-09.title: U.S. Weighing Future of Strategic Arms Pacts
2001-05-09.abstract: Military analysis; Pres Bush's decision to build missile defenses and eventually break with strategic arms control treaty, central treaty of cold war, is first outward sign of far-reaching debate over whether US should simply abandon business of negotiating strategic arms treaties; question centers on whether with end of cold war there is any need to begin yet another drawn-out arms control negotiation with Russia, which is no longer enemy (M)

2001-05-09.title: South Korea Scraps Military Exercise With Japan
2001-05-09.abstract: South Korea cancels military exercise with Japan and issues its strongest demand so far that Japan correct alleged distortions in schoolbook accounts of country's role on Korean Peninsula before and during World War II (S)

2001-05-09.title: Bush Presses Yugoslav Leader to Hand Over Milosevic
2001-05-09.abstract: WASHINGTON, May 9 - President Bush told Yugoslavia's president, Vojislav Kostunica, today that American financial assistance to Belgrade would depend on the fledgling democracy's cooperation with the war crimes tribunal at The Hague.

2001-05-09.title: The Farm Boy Sharon Recalls Struggle as a Way of Life
2001-05-09.abstract: Interview with Israel's Prime Min Ariel Sharon, who explains why he will not agree to freeze construction in existing Jewish settlements in West Bank and Gaza; says settlers need more room for growing families; rejects international view that settlement expansion is provocation to Palestinians or that existence and growth of settlements creates friction and causes conflict; photo (M)

2001-05-10.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: U.S. Shells Hit School
2001-05-10.abstract: Two projectiles fired by American Army Bradley fighting vehicle on training ground of military base in Germany crash into roof of nearby elementary school in Kirchenthumbach (S)

2001-05-10.title: Israel Strikes Targets in Gaza City
2001-05-10.abstract: JERUSALEM, May 10 — Two Romanian guest workers were killed by a bomb today as they were repairing the fence along the Israeli-Gazan border.

2001-05-10.title: World Briefing | Europe: Ireland: Date Set For Europe Vote
2001-05-10.abstract: Irish government sets date for referendum on treaty paving way for Central and Eastern European countries to join European Union (S)

2001-05-10.title: Bush Links Aid to Yugoslavia to the Extradition of Milosevic
2001-05-10.abstract: Pres Bush tells Pres Vojislav Kostunica of Yugoslavia that American financial aid to Belgrade would depend on cooperation with war crimes tribunal at The Hague; Kostunica, in visit to Washington, does not commit to handing over his predecessor, Slobodan Milosevic, but does say he is pushing to have his country's law changed so that Belgrade can cooperate with international tribunal investigating war crimes against ethnic Albanians in Kosovo in 1999; White House views Kostunica's remarks as first step and evolution in his thinking from month ago, when he refused outright to hand over Milosevic; but administration officials continue to press him to move further and allow foreign trial of Milosevic (M)

2001-05-10.title: 2 Jewish Teenagers Are Beaten to Death in the West Bank
2001-05-10.abstract: Two schoolboys from Jewish settlement on West Bank are found bludgeoned to death in cave; boys had skipped class to go hiking in nearby gorge; police say they were killed by Palestinians; Prime Min Ariel Sharon expresses 'deep shock' at deaths and calls them escalation of Palestinian terrorism against innocent civilians; accuses Palestinian Authority of failing to stop violence and of inciting murder in its official news media; police say two boys, Yaakov Mandel, 13, and Yosef Ishran, 14, were battered to death with rocks; say they believe boys died in apparent chance encounter with their attackers; deaths come two days after Palestinian baby girl is killed by Israeli tank fire, further roiling emotions in week of spiraling violence that neither side seems able to control; photos; map (M)

2001-05-11.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: New Pressure On Media Magnate
2001-05-11.abstract: Russian prosecutors give US officials documents to support charge of money laundering and money laundering by Vladimir Gusinsky, who Russians want extradited from US; photo (S)

2001-05-11.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Did The Doctor Kill More Than 400?
2001-05-11.abstract: British High Court inquiry in killings of patients by Manchester physician Harold Shipman will examine at least 466 deaths and seek information on 152 others; Shipman has been convicted of 15 murders (S)

2001-05-11.title: Soccer in Africa Once More Turns Deadly
2001-05-11.abstract: Ghana's official toll of 123 people dead and 93 injured in stampede at soccer match in Accra turns event into worst sporting disaster in Africa's history and pushes to nearly 180 number of spectators killed across continent in last month; deaths in stadium in Accra follow similar incidents in Ivory Coast, in Congo and in South Africa; in all cases, many accuse police of failing to control unruly crowds and, in some cases, making stampedes worse by firing excessive amounts of tear gas; deaths underscore how many African nations are unprepared to deal with security in stadiums, much less with rioting crowds; photo; map (M)

2001-05-11.title: U.S. Resident Jailed in China Needs Medical Care, Lawyers Say
2001-05-11.abstract: Liu Yaping, United States-based Chinese businessman who has been held for more than two months in Chinese military prison, is described as extremely ill by lawyers who are allowed to visit him for first time; lawyers claim he could die if he does not receive prompt medical treatment; Liu, who has green card and home in Connecticut, has been charged with only minor economic crime, but has been held incommunicado (M)

2001-05-11.title: World Briefing | Europe: Turkey: European Court Cites Abuses
2001-05-11.abstract: European Court of Human Rights finds Turkey guilty of widespread human rights abuses arising from 1974 invasion of northern Cyprus and 27-year occupation; case was brought by Cyprus government (S)

2001-05-12.title: German Parliament Votes To Revamp Pension System
2001-05-12.abstract: After years of deadlock on the issue, Germany's Parliament passed a ground-breaking law today aimed at overhauling the country's extremely generous but financially strained retirement system.    The new law tries to wean Germans from their almost total reliance on government pension programs that currently pay people 70 percent of their working income once they retire. The system has been an anchor to Germans, and most other European countries have similar systems. But all of these systems have been headed toward collapse, because the pool of younger workers paying in is being swamped by the  retirees collecting from it.

2001-05-12.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Ban On Catholic Priests Ends
2001-05-12.abstract: A law ending a 200-year-old ban on anyone who had ever been a Catholic priest from sitting in the House of Commons came into force as the campaign for the June 7 parliamentary elections began. The law will allow David Cairns, a Labor candidate who was a priest for three years, to take the seat in Northern Scotland he is favored to win. Anglican priests are already permitted to be members of Parliament.   Warren Hoge (NYT)

2001-05-12.title: David Jamieson, 80, Winner Of Top British Medal for Valor
2001-05-12.abstract: David Jamieson, who as British army captain received Victoria Cross, British Commonwealth's highest award for valor, for his role in repulsing German attack during breakout from D-Day invasion beaches in August 1944, dies at 80; photo (M)

2001-05-12.title: Talks Don't Calm Foes of Antimissile Plan
2001-05-12.abstract: After a week of consultations with allies and former adversaries, the Bush administration has failed to overcome deep concerns over whether its proposal to erect a broad array of missile defenses and abandon a major arms control treaty would undermine the strategic balance and promote an arms race.    An American team led by Deputy Defense Secretary Paul D. Wolfowitz was still wrapping up its meeting with top Foreign Ministry officials here today when the ministry spokesman, Aleksandr Yakovenko, announced that the American delegation had not addressed Russia's fundamental questions.

2001-05-13.title: In Ruin, Symbols on a Stone Hint at a Lost Asian Culture
2001-05-13.abstract: In an unexpected benefit of the cold war's end, Russian and American archaeologists say they have discovered an ancient civilization that thrived in Central Asia more than 4,000 years ago, before being lost in the sweep of history.    The people of that area, the archaeologists say, built oasis settlements with imposing mud-brick buildings and fortifications. They herded sheep and goats and grew wheat and barley in irrigated fields. They had bronze axes, fine ceramics, alabaster and bone carvings and jewelry of gold and semiprecious stones. They left luxury goods in the graves of an elite class.

2001-05-13.title: Lessons in Democracy, and Politics, in a Former Soviet Land
2001-05-13.abstract: When the history of Kazakhstan's stuttering transition from Soviet domination to independence is written, a snapshot of 19 people in the snow outside Parliament in the capital, Astana, may occupy an important place.     The picture commemorated something of a remarkable moment in this country, where Parliament is widely regarded as a rubber stamp.  The lower house had just rejected a bill that the president was pushing and that would have benefited his daughter's news media empire. The picture, taken in February, showed smiling people fresh from successful lobbying of the legislators.

2001-05-13.title: How Open to Immigrants Should Germany Be? An Uneasy Country's Debate Deepens
2001-05-13.abstract: With 7.3 million foreigners, or 9 percent of the population, Germany has the highest proportion of immigrants of any of the major European powers. But this presence makes many Germans uneasy and the country is now plunged once more into an intense debate about how open it should be.     The Christian Democrats this week opened a debate that is certain to be central to next year's election. ''Germany is not a classic country of immigration, and because of its history, geography and economic conditions, it cannot be one,'' the party said in a new policy paper.

2001-05-13.title: Sebastian Snow, Eccentric English Explorer, Dies at 72
2001-05-13.abstract: Sebastian Snow, eccentric English explorer who helped confirm source of Amazon River as he bumbled about vast portions of South America, died on April 20 at age 72; photo (M)

2001-05-13.title: Shipman's Victims
2001-05-13.abstract: The victims whose deaths led to convictions:

2001-05-14.title: Filipinos Vote on What's Seen as Referendum on President
2001-05-14.abstract: BANGKOK, May 14 — After rival "people power" demonstrations changed the face of Philippine politics in the last four months, Filipinos finally got a chance to vote today in a nationwide election that is being viewed as a referendum on the legitimacy of President Gloria Macapagal Arroyo.

2001-05-14.title: Talks Inching Ahead on Monitoring '72 Germ Warfare Pact
2001-05-14.abstract: Lengthy negotiations to fashion verification scheme for 29-year-old treaty banning biological weapons edge closer to agreement in Geneva; six-year negotiations are zeroeing in on half-dozen core areas where disagreement remains, with one more round in talks scheduled for July (M)

2001-05-14.title: Moderate Basque Nationalists Win Spanish Regional Election
2001-05-14.abstract: Basque nationalists win bitterly contested regional election, crushing hopes of mainstream Spanish parties to seize initiative in country's troubled northern region; voters reject warnings from national government that victory for moderate nationalists would mean support for armed separatist group ETA; party wins 42.7 percent of vote, which will give it 33 seats in 75-member regional parliament; Juan Jose Ibarretxe is elected head of government; photo (M)

2001-05-14.title: IN ITALIAN BALLOT, THE CONSERVATIVES EDGE TOWARD RULE
2001-05-14.abstract: Election results suggest Silvio Berlusconi, conservative media baron who promises to do for Italy what he has done for his business, may be headed for victory in general election and his second chance to form new government; coalition candidates lead in upper and lowerhouses of Parliament; victory over former Rome Mayor Francesco Rutelli would bring end to five years of center-left government and mark spirited return to power for businessman-turned-politician, whose first center-right government collapsed in 1994 after seven months; photos (M)

2001-05-14.title: Fiery Actress Helps Opposition Strengthen Role in India
2001-05-14.abstract: Jayalalitha Jayaram, flamboyant politician convicted recently for corruption, leads alliance of parties to landslide victory in elections in Tamil Nadu, India, winning state with heavy support from women and rural poor; triumph underscores that corruption is not decisive issue with electorate that regards most politicians as corrupt anyway; ensures that 24 years of unbroken rule in Tamil Nadu by Communist Party of India will continue; photo (M)

2001-05-15.title: Tale of the Tories: The Leader Under Fire
2001-05-15.abstract: Conservative William Hague is attracting most attention in Britain's election campaign, even though he is candidate everyone expects to lose; press has been unsparing in pointing up his ineffectiveness as leader of beleaguered Tories; not even widely praised opening of his election campaign and contrastingly poor one by rival, Prime Min Tony Blair, managed to budge polls, which show Tories still trailing by up to 20 points (M)

2001-05-15.title: Japan's Princess Is Pregnant
2001-05-15.abstract: TOKYO, 15 May — The Imperial Household Agency officially confirmed today that Crown Princess Masako is pregnant.

2001-05-15.title: North Korea May Get Help Under Plan By Europeans
2001-05-15.abstract: European Union announces it will open diplomatic relations with North Korea to help Pyongyang solve its food and health crisis; says it will support peace efforts between two Koreas (S)

2001-05-15.title: New President Tested by Vote in Philippines
2001-05-15.abstract: Filipinos vote in general elections seen as referendum on legitimacy of Pres Gloria Macapagal Arroyo, who ousted Joseph Estrada from office in January with help from military; election signifies return to constitutional process after upheavals that showed how tenuous country's democracy continues to be; photo (M)

2001-05-15.title: Moderate Basques Promise To Focus on Peace Talks
2001-05-15.abstract: Moderate Basque nationalists, who won bitterly fought regional election in Spain on May 13, begin efforts to form new government with aim of ending violence by ETA, radical separatist group; Basque Nationalist Party won 33 seats in 75-member regional parliament in Vitoria, one more than center-right Popular Party of Prime Min Jose Maria Aznar and opposition Socialist Party combined; Nationalist leader Xabier Arzalluz says bringing end to killing is issue, rather than achieving greater sovereignty for autonomous region (M)

2001-05-16.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Israel: Palestinian Bus Driver Convicted
2001-05-16.abstract: Khalil Abu Elba, Palestinian bus driver, is convicted of murder for plowing his bus into crowd and killing eight Israelies; photo (S)

2001-05-16.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Israel: General's Trial Begins
2001-05-16.abstract: Israeli prosecutors begin trial of Itzhak Yaakov, 75-year-old retired general accused of divulging classified information related to Israel's nuclear weapons program; he is charged with endangering national security (S)

2001-05-16.title: World Briefing | United Nations: Proposal For West Africa
2001-05-16.abstract: United Nations officials recommend setting up new operations office for West Africa, recognizing that civil war in Sierra Leone has mushroomed into region-wide crisis (S)

2001-05-16.title: Islamic Hard-Liners in Iran Cut Election Candidates to 10
2001-05-16.abstract: TEHRAN, May 16 — The Council of Guardians, which screens candidates for Iranian elections, announced today that it was rejecting all but 10 of the more than 800 men and women who sought to run in the June 8 presidential race. In addition to the incumbent, Mohammad Khatami, the list of those approved included the defense minister, Adm. Ali Shamkani; a former minister of intelligence, Ali Fallahian; a former labor minister, Ahmed Tavakoli; and Abdollah Jasbi, the chancellor of the Islamic Open University.

2001-05-16.title: U.N. Delegation, Off to Africa, Sees Glimmer of Hope for Congo
2001-05-16.abstract: Most of Security Council leaves for Africa for intense round of talks intended to speed end of war in Congo; unusually large delegation will meet with leaders in at least nine nations; French Amb Jean-David Levitte, who leads mission, comments, interview (M)

2001-05-17.title: Bush Is Likely to Meet Putin During June Visit to Europe
2001-05-17.abstract: United States officials say Pres Bush will probably meet Russian Pres Vladimir N Putin during June visit to Europe; topics will include friction over US missile defense plans and Bush's reluctance to provide added financial aid to Russia (M)

2001-05-17.title: Cramped Singapore Military Relies on Space Elsewhere
2001-05-17.abstract: SINGAPORE—  Whenever Tan Kah Han, a lieutenant colonel in the Singapore Air Force, takes off to the north for training in his F-16C jet fighter, he faces an immediate problem: Climbing at 540 kilometers per hour, he has barely 45 seconds to avoid crossing the border with Malaysia, less than three kilometers away.

2001-05-17.title: Ukraine Calls Newsman's Killing Nonpolitical; Skeptics Abound
2001-05-17.abstract: Ukraine Pres Leonid Kuchma's critics scorn government's stand that leading opposition journalist Georgy Gongadze was victim of random killing by two thugs who were themselves killed later; government has been in crisis since former presidential guard released tape showing Kuchma ordering underlings to deal with Gongadze (M)

2001-05-17.title: Spotlight (Finally) Fades on Marcos Wealth
2001-05-17.abstract: MANILA—  She is best known as the woman who loved to flaunt it — diamond-encrusted jewelry, hand-tailored outfits from the best names in Western fashion and, of course, those thousands of pairs of designer shoes.

2001-05-18.title: World Briefing | Europe: Chechnya: Capital's Mayor Quits
2001-05-18.abstract: Bislan Gantamirov resigns as mayor of Grozny, Chechnya's ruined capital, after criticizing Russian authorities' failure to re-establish order (S)

2001-05-18.title: 25 Years Later, Rumsfeld's Dream Is Alive Again
2001-05-18.abstract: Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld will tell Congress that armed forces need infusion of money to meet foreign threats, just as he did 25 years ago when he served in same post during Ford administration; Rumsfeld will make case for adding billions of dollars to current defense budget and increasing Pres Bush's proposed $324 billion Pentagon budget; his goal is to transform military into more agile, lethal and stealthy force, and to build costly and unproven missile shield; says Americans may feel safer today than in decades, but there is danger in complacency; critics contend Rumsfeld and Bush's other top advisers are exaggerating military challenges facing US and that he is arguing for missile shield at time when, at least numerically, missile threat has lessened; Rumsfeld cites China and North Korea as potential threats to US; he acknowledges he has tough selling job ahead; photos (M)

2001-05-18.title: French Report Faults Response to Mad Cow Crisis
2001-05-18.abstract: Parliamentary committee issues scathing report on France's handling of mad cow crisis during last 10 years; accuses successive agriculture ministers of blocking precautionary steps because they were trying to appease powerful agriculture sector (S)

2001-05-18.title: Bush and Putin Agree to Meet in Slovenia Next Month
2001-05-18.abstract: WASHINGTON, May 18 — Meetings today between Russian Foreign Minister Igor S. Ivanov, President Bush and other senior administration officials resulted in an agreement to hold a summit meeting next month in Slovenia, administration officials said.

2001-05-19.title: World Briefing | Europe: Vatican: Quiet Birthday, With A Treat
2001-05-19.abstract: Pope John Paul II turned 81 yesterday without fanfare, spending a routine day at the Vatican that included meetings with new ambassadors, Pakistani bishops and an audience for 120 members of the European Automobile Manufacturers Association. The pope, who will preside over a gathering of cardinals next week, did receive a birthday torte at a small lunch with Vatican aides.     Alessandra Stanley (NYT)

2001-05-19.title: Brazil, Fearful of Blackouts, Orders 20% Cut in Electricity
2001-05-19.abstract: Faced with a rapidly worsening energy crisis that threatens to cripple Latin America's largest economy, the government ordered consumers and businesses today to cut electricity consumption by 20 percent or prepare for California-style blackouts.    The reduction is to be achieved through rationing that will start on June 1 and includes surcharges for those who do not meet their cutback quotas and rewards for those who do. The restrictions are to remain for at least six months, officials said.

2001-05-19.title: French Oil Executive Calls Commissions Routine
2001-05-19.abstract: A former head of France's state-owned oil company, now on trial for corruption, has told two French newspapers that paying millions of dollars a year in ''secret commissions'' was a routine part of his job and had the approval of the Elysée Palace.     The executive -- Loïk Le Floch-Prigent, who headed Elf Aquitaine from 1989 to 1993 -- said that the payments were made to several African governments as well as to Spain and Germany, and that perhaps as many as 40 people in the French government knew of the payments.

2001-05-19.title: Bush and Putin Agree to Meet in Slovenia
2001-05-19.abstract: President Bush and Russia's president, Vladimir V. Putin, will meet for the first time in Slovenia on June 16 at the end of a European trip by Mr. Bush, the White House said today.     The site of the meeting, in a tiny country once part of Yugoslavia, was symbolically important for the United States because Slovenia is a prime candidate to join NATO.

2001-05-19.title: World Briefing | Africa: Libya: Nearly 100 Migrants Die In Desert
2001-05-19.abstract: The bodies of at least 93 African migrants were found in the Sahara in southern Libya near the Niger border, according to the state news agency. Crossing the Sahara is one of the most popular and dangerous smuggling routes for economic migrants from West and Central Africa, who want to work in Libya or use it as a springboard to Europe. They travel in old, overloaded trucks that often break down during the long journey through the desert.     Norimitsu Onishi (NYT)

2001-05-20.title: W.H.O. Says More Donations Can Bring TB Under Control
2001-05-20.abstract: World Health Organization sets drive to combat tuberculosis; says disease could be brought under control within 5 years if nations would commit added $400 million a year; says added funds would finance treatment for 70 percent of those infected, up from current 23 percent, and halve number of deaths from disease by end of decade; warns deaths will otherwise double, to 10.2 million a year (M)

2001-05-20.title: Russia to Begin Overhaul of Electricity Industry
2001-05-20.abstract: Russia tentatively sets sweeping electricity industry overhaul; plans to divide sector into generation, distribution and sales units and sell stakes in units, while retaining full control over grid itself, and thousands of miles of power lines; plan's critics, including Prime Min Vladimir Putin's economic adviser, Andrei Illarionov, say it does not adequately safeguard current electric company owners (M)

2001-05-20.title: Cardinals Campaign, Very Delicately, for Pope
2001-05-20.abstract: Planned College of Cardinals consistory, first since 1994, will bring together all 134 men under 80 who are eligible to vote in papal conclave; gives Pope John Paul II's advisers chance to campaign, delicately, to succeed him; possible contenders noted; photos (M)

2001-05-20.title: Taliban's Ban On Poppy A Success, U.S. Aides Say
U.S. Germ Warfare Review Faults Plan on Enforcement
2001-05-20.abstract: Confidential Bush administration review reportedly recommends United States not accept draft accord to enforce 1972 treaty banning germ weapons; accord, known as protocol, sets steps to monitor ban; treaty, which bars development, production and possession of biological arms lacks means of verifying compliance; Bush team found protocol would not stop cheating and that deficiencies could not be remedied by negotiating deadline; review, certain to distress allies, is emerging as sensitive diplomatic problem; table of biological weapons banned under treaty (M)

2001-05-21.title: New Yorker Has Low Hopes for New Peru Trial
2001-05-21.abstract: Interview with Lori Berenson, native New Yorker convicted in 1996 by secret military antiterrorism court in Peru and sentenced to life in prison, who is being retried; she is accused of playing leading role in plot by Tupac Amaru Revolutionary Movement to take entire Congress hostage; photo (M)

2001-05-21.title: St. Patrick Has His Day, 2 Months Late
2001-05-21.abstract: St Patrick's Day is celebrated in Dublin, Ireland, after two-month delay because of outbreak of foot-and-mouth disease (S)

2001-05-21.title: Peru Ex-President, Trailing, Links Election Foe to Cocaine Use
2001-05-21.abstract: Former president Alan Garcia and Alejandro Toledo, candidates in Peru's presidential election, engage in televised debate; Garcia, who trails in polls, tries to portray Toledo as cocaine user; Toledo attacks Garcia for his 1985-90 term in office, which is widely remembered for corruption, hyperinflation, terrorism, army massacres and food rationing (S)

2001-05-21.title: Fear of Ethnic Attacks Grips Macedonia's Cities
2001-05-21.abstract: Fear grips Macedonia's cities, notably capital of Skopje, that three-month-old conflict between Macedonian security forces and ethnic Albanian rebels in mountainous border areas could spill over into urban areas and develop into ethnic violence; both sides fault coalition unity government, accusing it of not moving fast enough to end conflict; Macedonians prefer quick military solution; Albanians are demanding constitutional changes that would put them on equal footing with Macedonians and remove source of protest; map; photo (M)

2001-05-21.title: When a Tough Sharon Gets Tougher, Israelis Get Nervous
2001-05-21.abstract: First real domestic criticism of Prime Min Ariel Sharon surges in aftermath of first use of American-supplied F-16 fighter jets in Israeli-Palestinian conflict, with questions about whether government has any plan beyond its next retaliation; four Israeli dailies, including conservative Jerusalem Post, condemn as unwise deadly warplane attack against Nablus police station in response to suicide bombing by Hamas militant in Netanya; while many Israelis applaud Sharon for finally unleashing Israel's most deadly weapons against Palestinians, others question wisdom of action with potential to ignite conflict and put Israel on defensive against international criticism; many ask whether use of warplanes demonstrates that government has neither military nor diplomatic answer to conflict; photo (M)

2001-05-22.title: World Briefing | Asia: Mongolia: Ex-Communist Re-elected
2001-05-22.abstract: Mongolian Pres Natsagiin Bagabandi is re-elected by large majority, further consolidating return of reformed version of Communist Party, which once ruled country with tight grip; photo (S)

2001-05-22.title: U.S. Negotiates a Reduction of Its Dues to Support W.H.O.
2001-05-22.abstract: GENEVA, May 22 - The United States today won a reduction in its budgetary contributions to the World Health Organization, although the head of the United Nations agency warned that some health programs could suffer as a result.

2001-05-22.title: Albanian Rebels to Quit Serbia This Week
2001-05-22.abstract: Albanian rebels prepare to leave three-mile-wide buffer zone in Presevo Valley in southern Serbia by May 24; area will be reoccupied by Yugoslav Army; rebel commander Muhamet Xhemajli comments; map; photo (M)

2001-05-22.title: Bush Talks With Dalai Lama Seen by China as 2d Jab in Eye
2001-05-22.abstract: P> BEIJING, May 22 — It is just "a coincidence," of course, that President Bush will have a well publicized "private meeting" with the Dalai Lama on, of all days, May 23.

2001-05-22.title: Tad Szulc, 74, Dies; Times Correspondent Who Uncovered Bay of Pigs Imbroglio
2001-05-22.abstract: Tad Szulc, former foreign correspondent for The New York Times whose reports of imminent assault on Cuba by anti-Castro Cubans in 1961 came to reality in disastrous Bay of Pigs invasion, dies at age 74; photo (M)

2001-05-23.title: ISRAEL TELLS ARMY TO WITHHOLD FIRE UNLESS IN DANGER
2001-05-23.abstract: Israel reports ordering troops to 'cease fire' unless in danger; unexpected order seems to be effort to seize initiative in responding to new American-led international offensive to halt violence; Prime Min Ariel Sharon did not disclose new policy in rare television speech earlier in evening; repeated his view that Palestinians switched to violence eight months ago and his appeal that they abandon it; both Israel and Palestinians say they accept report of international fact-finding committee as basis for braking disintegration of their relationship; Palestinians score Israel's rejection of total freeze on settlement construction; Sharon says Israel will not seize new land to expand Jewish communities in West Bank and Gaza; graph of Mideast death toll; photo (M)

2001-05-23.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Israel: Inquiry In Reporter's Shooting
2001-05-23.abstract: Israeli Justice Ministry investigators are collecting evidence on shooting by Isr4

2001-05-23.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Chancellor's Secret Cousin
2001-05-23.abstract: German Chancellor Gerhard Schroder has first cousin, identified as Renate G., who worked for Stasi, intelligence service of former East Germany; Schroder says he has met Renate and forgiven her (S)

2001-05-23.title: World Briefing | Asia: China: Extradition Deal Proposed
2001-05-23.abstract: China pledges to Canada that it will not execute two Chinese fugitives if they are extradited to face charges in country's largest smuggling case; one fugitive, Lai Changxing, is accused of masterminding multi-billion-dollar operation that has resulted in executions of more than 10 officials (S)

2001-05-23.title: Siberian Ice Jam Bombed
2001-05-23.abstract: Russian air force bombs stubborn mass of ice floes that have backed up Lena River in Siberia, flooding town of Lensk and inundating part of Yakutsk, in worst flooding in area in 100 years; new crest of floodwaters moves toward Yakutsk (S)

2001-05-24.title: U.N. Delegation Finds Reasons for Hope in Congo Peace Talks
2001-05-24.abstract: Twelve Security Council ambassadors find positive developments in their visit to Congo and neighboring countries to encourage peaceful resolution of war there; Amb Jean-David Levitte of France, leader of group. says all major foreign participants in Congo's war are committed to withdrawal, and Congolese rebels are also ready to disarm (M)

2001-05-24.title: Powell Begins Africa Tour With Praise for Developments in Mali
2001-05-24.abstract: Secretary of State Colin L Powell begins weeklong visit to Africa with stop in Mali, one of West Africa's zones of stability; Powell holds up Mali as example of what struggling African continent can become (M)

2001-05-24.title: A Father's Cranky Essays on Web Site Put Son in Jail in China
2001-05-24.abstract: Chinese government's frantic attempt to control political views on Internet takes new twist with detention of Hu Dalin, man whose only apparent trespass is to help his father, Lu Jiaping, maintain Web sites featuring father's obscure, left-wing writings; father, who knows little about computers, remains free, but son, who dabbles in computers, was jailed May 18 (M)

2001-05-24.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: Monkey-Man? Relax, Relax
2001-05-24.abstract: Fear that gripped Delhi, India, over monkey-man, creature many believed was half man, half monkey, has eased as police and medical association call reports of sightings mass hysteria; three people have died in falls leaping from rooftops to escape beast; drawing (S)

2001-05-24.title: Paper's Executive Shot Dead; Attack Tied to Basque Rebels
2001-05-24.abstract: MARDID, May 24 — A Spanish newspaper executive was shot dead today in an attack that the police said was the work of the Basque separatist group E.T.A. It was the first fatal incident said to involve the rebels since Basques voted overwhelmingly on May 13 against their political allies.

2001-05-25.title: World Briefing | Europe: Ireland: Access To Adoption Records
2001-05-25.abstract: Ireland will permit adopted people over age 18 to gain access to birth certificates, and allow birth parents of adopted children to find information on them (S)

2001-05-25.title: A Balkan Day: One Step Forward and Three Steps Back
2001-05-25.abstract: Prominent and popular Albanian rebel commander known as Leshi is shot dead close to Serbian forces' positions just hours after Serbia's prime minister, Zoran Djindjic, sits with ethnic Albanian villagers in Muhovac, telling them not to be scared of Serbian police; photo (M)

2001-05-25.title: World Briefing | Europe: Vatican: Pope's Message To Cardinals
2001-05-25.abstract: Pope John Paul II concludes meeting of cardinals, some of whom have called for more power sharing; pope tells prelates that existing systems are effective (S)

2001-05-25.title: World Briefing | Africa: Sudan: Government Declares Bombing Halt
2001-05-25.abstract: Sudan government will halt bombings in south; announcement comes as Sec Colin Powell arrives in region to discuss 18-year civil war (S)

2001-05-26.title: Soldiers in Georgia Stage a Brief Near Mutiny
2001-05-26.abstract: As many as 400 Georgian national guardsmen left their barracks today and took over a police station outside the capital, Tbilisi, to protest long delays in paychecks and other economic hardships, briefly raising fears of a coup.    But late today, after face-to-face talks with the soldiers, President Eduard Shevardnadze of Georgia said that their protest was economic, not political, and that the soldiers would end their near mutiny on Saturday. He called the action a grave crime, but said he did not support prosecuting the troops.

2001-05-26.title: India's Leader Speaks of Reconciliation in Letter to Pakistan
2001-05-26.abstract: In a graceful letter, Prime Minister Atal Behari Vajpayee today officially invited Pakistan's military ruler, Gen. Pervez Musharraf, to India for the first talks between the two nations since an earlier peace program was ruptured by a violent conflict in Kashmir two years ago.    ''Our common enemy is poverty,'' Mr. Vajpayee wrote. ''For the welfare of our peoples, there is no other recourse but a pursuit of the path of reconciliation.''

2001-05-26.title: World Briefing | Europe: Ukraine: Government Backtracks On Slaying
2001-05-26.abstract: Ten days after proclaiming that the government had solved the killing of the opposition journalist Georgy Gongadze, Interior Minister Yuri Smirnov said he had spoken prematurely and was only expressing a personal opinion about the case. The killing has caused a long-running crisis for President Leonid D. Kuchma; secretly recorded audiotapes released by a former bodyguard appear to show Mr. Kuchma ordering Mr. Gongadze's kidnapping. Mr. Kuchma has denied any role.     Michael Wines (NYT)

2001-05-26.title: Currais Novos Journal; Pity the Donkey, a Beast That's Become a Burden
2001-05-26.abstract: Consider the humble donkey. Hard-working, reliable, docile, it has been prized for centuries in the parched and barren northeast of Brazil as both beast of burden and loyal companion.    But no longer. Suddenly an animal once celebrated in song and even honored with statues in town squares has become an unwelcome presence -- spurned, disdained and discarded by the same peasant farmers who until recently could not have earned a living without ''the poor man's car.''

2001-05-27.title: In Kenya, Powell Repeats Call for New Generation of Leaders
2001-05-27.abstract: Sec Colin Powell meets with Kenya's longtime leader, Daniel arap Moi, urging him to step aside after elections next year; also calls for anti-corruption law and resumption of ties with IMF and World Bank; Powell, making Africa tour, has voiced belief that some leaders there have clung to power too long (M)

2001-05-27.title: An Aging Ex-Dictator Who Refuses to Recant
2001-05-27.abstract: Gen Wojciech Jaruzelski, old soldier who was once Poland's military dictator, is on trial along with eight other former military, police and Communist officials on charges of manslaughter for allegedly giving orders that led to shooting deaths of 44 protesting workers in Gdansk in 1970; Jaruzelski, unrepentant, says those who are comfortable now should remember what that time was like, insisting actions were necessary to avoid Soviet invasion; his photo (M)

2001-05-27.title: Trying to Tell a Truce From a War
2001-05-27.abstract: Armenian-Azerbaijani war over Nagorno-Karabakh, which has raged on and off since 1988, has destroyed Azerbaijani city of Aghdam, driving many thousands of people out of homes and further calcified enmities that stretch back more than thousand years; photo; map; leaders of both nations are loath to suggest compromises required for peace as negotiations brokered by US, Russia and France try to outline some form of agreement (M)

2001-05-27.title: U.N. Expert: Crucial Battle Against AIDS Is Prevention
2001-05-27.abstract: UNITED NATIONS, May 25 — Peter Piot, the epidemiologist who has been coordinating an international campaign against AIDS since 1995, is spending less time worrying about the price of drugs and more about the shortage of condoms.

2001-05-27.title: In Old-Time Boss's Redoubt, Mexico Tests Political Wind
2001-05-27.abstract: Election in Mexico's Yucatan, stronghold of longtime political boss Victor Cervera, will test whether Pres Vicente Fox's victory was truly tidal wave of change; Cervera has used every means, legal and otherwise, to delay vote and keep out observers to protect hand-picked successor Orlando Paredesfrom challenge of Patricio Patron; photos; map (M)

2001-05-28.title: SILENT PLAGUE: A special report.; Deadly Shadow Darkens Remote Chinese Village
2001-05-28.abstract: AIDS virus has devastated impoverished and remote Donghu, China, where some 80 percent of adults carry HIV and over 60 percent are already debilitated; depth of tragedy in villages like Donghu, something officials in Henan Province have generally insisted is not problem, is only now emerging as desperate, dying farmers have started to speak out; many farmers in province were infected in 1990's at government-owned collection stations where they sold their blood; virus has continued to spread through other routes because officials have blocked research and education drives; map; photos (M)

2001-05-28.title: U.S. WILL OFFER AID TO MOSCOW IN PLAN TO UNDO ABM PACT
2001-05-28.abstract: Bush administration strategists say United States will offer Russia arms purchases, military aid and joint antimissile exercises to win its cooperation in scrapping 1972 Antiballistic Missile Treaty; say deal will likely include offer to buy Russian-made S-300 surface-to-air missiles that could be integrated into defensive shield over Russia and Europe; plan reflects Pres Bush's need for Pres Vladimir V Putin's consent to scrap treaty, both to defuse European objections and to satisfy Congress (M)

2001-05-28.title: Russia Takes Advantage of Brain Power at Home
2001-05-28.abstract: ST. PETERSBURG—  This city has earned a number of nicknames in its 300-year history — Venice of the North, the Northern Palmir and Russia's Cultural Capital. Now St. Petersburg has joined the growing list of places trying to add a "Silicon Valley" nickname.

2001-05-28.title: Letters to the Technology Editor : Manual Labor
2001-05-28.abstract: We have been going bananas trying to find a manual typewriter in Paris, London and Rome! The Olivetti shop near the Vatican only had Italian keyboards. Who wants an Italian keyboard? We can't afford to fly to New York to get the Olivetti Lettera 25 or 35. And my present Lettera 25 is failing fast.

2001-05-28.title: PRI Opponent Claims Victory in Yucatán Election

2001-05-29.title: World Briefing | Asia: Pakistan: Help For Drug Addicts
2001-05-29.abstract: United Nations Drug Control Program will provide over $500,000 for network of 17 drug rehabilitation centers in Pakistan, nation with some 4.5 million addicts (S)

2001-05-29.title: World Briefing | Americas: Chile: Book Him, Court Says
2001-05-29.abstract: Chilean appeals court rules Gen Augusto Pinochet should be fingerprinted and photographed by police as he awaits possible trial on charges of human rights violations (S)

2001-05-29.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Iraq: Absolved Of U.N. Deaths
2001-05-29.abstract: Fuad Hussein Haidar, gunman who attacked Baghdad office of United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization in 2000, is absolved of guilt for slaying of deputy director and computer expert, but sentenced to 10 years in prison for attempted killing of Iraqi guards outside building; said he wanted to avenge deaths of Iraqis who perished under UN sanctions (S)

2001-05-29.title: World Briefing | Europe: Chad: Re-election, And Arrests
2001-05-29.abstract: Chad electoral commission says Pres Idriss Deby was re-elected with 67 percent of votes; six other candidates, who charged widespread fraud, are arrested during meeting at one of their homes and held briefly; security forces shoot in air to disperse supporters, some of whom are reportedly hurt in resulting panic; photo (S)

2001-05-29.title: World Briefing | Europe: Vatican: Regret Over Archbishop's Marriage
2001-05-29.abstract: Vatican expresses 'deep regret' over marriage of Zambian Abp Emanuel Milingo, 71, to Korean woman, 43, in group rite performed by Rev Sun Myung Moon; warns Milingo faces possible excommunication (S)

2001-05-30.title: Unrest Erupts on Eve of Session on Indonesia Leader's Future
2001-05-30.abstract: Violence erupts in Indonesia on eve of legislative session aimed at removing Pres Abdurrahman Wahid from power because; armed loyalists burn churches in his home province of Pasuruan and descend on Jakarta; maps (M)

2001-05-30.title: Sharon Castigates Israelis for Skirting Regulations
2001-05-30.abstract: JERUSALEM, May 30 — Prime Minister Ariel Sharon delivered an unusual address to the Parliament today in which he attacked as self-destructive one of the guiding principles of Israeli life: thou shalt not be a sucker.

2001-05-30.title: Professed Market Reformer Becomes Ukraine's Prime Minister
2001-05-30.abstract: Ukraine's Parliament confirms Anatoly Kinakh, lobbyist and ally of Pres Leonid D Kuchma, as prime minister, quieting political crisis that has steadily sapped Kuchma's popularity and power; he Kinakh pledges to press ahead with Kuchma's agenda of rebuilding political and economic systems along Western lines (M)

2001-05-30.title: NATO Is Unyielding On Bush Missile Plan
2001-05-30.abstract: In his first effort to sell President Bush's missile defense plan to allies, Secretary of State Colin L. Powell failed to pierce NATO's opposition.    At a meeting of foreign ministers, Secretary Powell pressed the administration's case for abandoning the 1972 Antiballistic Missile Treaty and shifting American doctrine away from the cold war theory of mutually assured destruction.

2001-05-30.title: World Briefing | Asia: China: Taiwan Businessman Executed
2001-05-30.abstract: China executes Taiwanese businessma, Cao Yufei, for his role in big investment scam (S)

2001-05-31.title: A Colombian Human Rights Figure Is the New Defense Minister
2001-05-31.abstract: Gustavo Bell is chosen to replace Luis Fernando Ramirez as Colombia's minister of defense; Bell, as vice president, had directed government's human rights efforts; Ramirez may run for president in next year's election; he is credited with overseeing modernization of Colombian Army; speculation centers on whether Bell's appointment is signal to military officials about need to cut links that human rights groups say army has with right-wing paramilitary forces; but several human rights officials say Bell has little to show for his human rights efforts, and note that military officials have welcomed his new appointment (M)

2001-05-31.title: World Briefing | Europe: Northern Ireland: Inspectors View I.R.A. Arms
2001-05-31.abstract: International arms monitors say they carried out third inspection of secret Irish Republican Army weapons dumps and found them secure and untouched (S)

2001-05-31.title: British-U.S. Plan to Ease Curb On Iraq Seems Stalled for Now
2001-05-31.abstract: Proposal by United States and Britain to loosen restrictions on trade with Iraq while tightening controls against oil smuggling and illegal arms sales is meeting strong opposition in Security Council and is unlikely to be adopted soon; French, Chinese and Russian diplomats say deadline for reaching agreement cannot be met because of problems caused by lengthy list of items US wants to bar Iraq from buying without approval; say banning some of items on list could hinder any revival of Iraq's economy (M)

2001-05-31.title: This Time, 80's Populist Sounds Capitalist Theme in Peru
2001-05-31.abstract: Alan Garcia, former president of Peru, is running for second term by apologizing for mistakes he made in his first term; when he left office in 1990, terrorism raged across country, consumers waited on long lines for food and government was corrupt and virtually bankrupt; Garcia says he has learned from his experience and wants to put improve his standing in Peruvian history books; he is in runoff with Alejandro Toledo, who has long been overwhelming favorite to win presidency until recent weeks, before reports that he used cocaine, associated with prostitutes, laundered campaign contributions and abandoned illegitimate daughter; latest opinion polls show Garcia only 4 percent behind Toledo; photo; map (M)

2001-05-31.title: In Ravaged English City, Racial Mix Was Volatile
2001-05-31.abstract: Theories abound about underlying reasons for what is being called worst race riots in Britain in 15 years, in depressed former mill town of Oldham; some blame pervasive racism among whites, who make up 89 percent of population in Oldham; others blame increasing lawlessness among youths of Pakistani and Bangladeshi origin, who reportedly declared whole swaths of Oldham off limits to whites; many point to inflammatory presence of British nationalist and white supremacist groups, who have been distributing pro-white leaflets and spreading anti-immigrant messages since April, after elderly war veteran was beaten up by several nonwhite youths; photos; map (M)
